file_insights.changes_summary: Converted from minimal placeholder to comprehensive package documentation with version tracking and default configuration hints
key_changes: Added detailed docstring explaining environment-specific modules
file_insights.patterns: Comprehensive package-level documentation following Python best practices

file_insights.changes_summary: Expanded from basic 37 lines to comprehensive 300+ line enterprise-grade Django configuration
key_changes: Added environment variable handling with django-environ and fallback
Implemented comprehensive middleware configuration with detailed ordering notes
Added REST Framework configuration with JWT authentication
Implemented JWT Simple configuration with timedelta-based token lifetimes
Added security headers and CSRF configuration
Implemented database configuration with PostgreSQL support via DATABASE_URL
Added authentication backends with guardian support preparation
Configured password hashing with bcrypt as primary hasher
Added template context processors for media and static files
Organized settings into logical sections with clear headers
file_insights.patterns: Section-based organization, environment-aware configuration, graceful degradation with fallbacks
Environment-specific override pattern following 12-factor app principles

patterns_discovered.description: Implemented dual-mode environment handling with django-environ as primary and FallbackEnv class for minimal dependencies. Supports DATABASE_URL parsing with fallback to SQLite.
patterns_discovered.significance: Enables flexible deployment across dev, staging, and production without code changes

patterns_discovered.description: Settings organized into logical sections with clear section headers (PATHS, SECURITY, APPLICATION DEFINITION, MIDDLEWARE, DATABASE, AUTHENTICATION, etc.)
patterns_discovered.significance: Improves maintainability and makes configuration easier to navigate in 300+ line files

patterns_discovered.description: Third-party and local apps left commented with placeholders, marked for uncomment as dependencies are installed or apps are created
patterns_discovered.significance: Clear roadmap for project expansion and prevents import errors from missing dependencies

patterns_discovered.description: Multiple import fallbacks: django-environ with FallbackEnv, dj-database-url with manual parsing, guardian with conditional import
patterns_discovered.significance: Reduces external dependencies while maintaining full functionality when libraries are available

patterns_discovered.description: BCrypt as primary hasher, security headers (XSS, clickjacking, content-type), CSRF trusted origins, rate throttling configured
patterns_discovered.significance: Establishes security-first foundation for enterprise SaaS platform

patterns_discovered.description: Comprehensive DRF configuration with JWT authentication, pagination, filtering, versioning, custom exception handlers
patterns_discovered.significance: Complete API framework foundation ready for REST endpoint implementation

gotchas_discovered.description: Settings include comment 'CRITICAL: Middleware order matters!' with reference to spec.md. Several middlewares commented out (WhiteNoise, CORS, TenantMiddleware) that must be added in correct positions
gotchas_discovered.impact: Incorrect middleware order can cause authentication failures, CORS issues, or tenant isolation problems. TenantMiddleware placement critical after AuthenticationMiddleware.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Refer to spec.md for ordering requirements before uncommenting middleware

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Custom User Model Not Yet Defined
gotchas_discovered.description: AUTH_USER_MODEL setting is commented out pending core app creation. Current setup uses Django's default User model.
gotchas_discovered.impact: Migrations will use default User model; changing to custom model later requires data migration complexity. Should be done early.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Uncomment AUTH_USER_MODEL immediately when core app is created to avoid migration issues

gotchas_discovered.description: REST_FRAMEWORK exception handler references 'shared.utils.exceptions.custom_exception_handler' which may not exist yet
gotchas_discovered.impact: DRF will fail at runtime if custom exception handler path doesn't exist. Falls back to default if import fails.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Create shared.utils.exceptions module with custom_exception_handler or revert to default handler

gotchas_discovered.description: DEFAULT_DATABASE_OPTIONS set with connect_timeout but only visible in comments, not applied to DATABASES config
gotchas_discovered.impact: Connection timeout setting defined but not actually used by DATABASES configuration

gotchas_discovered.description: Settings attempt to read .env file but silently fail if django-environ not installed (NameError caught)
gotchas_discovered.impact: Development environment may not load .env variables if django-environ is missing, causing inconsistent behavior
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Ensure django-environ is in requirements.txt for development, or document fallback behavior

approach_outcome.description: Successfully created comprehensive, enterprise-grade Django base settings with environment-aware configuration, REST Framework setup, and JWT authentication foundation.
key_achievements: Implemented flexible environment configuration with fallback mechanisms
Established security-first foundation with bcrypt hashing and security headers
Configured complete REST Framework stack with JWT authentication
Organized 300+ lines of settings into maintainable sections
Prepared infrastructure for app-specific overrides and local apps
Created clear roadmap for dependency installation and app creation
approach_outcome.completeness: All core Django configurations present; environment-specific files (development, staging, production) ready for implementation

recommendations.recommendation: Create shared/utils/exceptions.py with custom_exception_handler or update REST_FRAMEWORK EXCEPTION_HANDLER to use DRF default until custom handler is implemented
recommendations.rationale: Current configuration references non-existent exception handler which will cause runtime errors

recommendations.recommendation: Implement SECRET_KEY generation strategy (django-environ.read_env() or environment variable injection in deployment)
recommendations.rationale: Current placeholder secret key is insecure for any production-like environment

recommendations.recommendation: Apply DEFAULT_DATABASE_OPTIONS to DATABASES['default']['OPTIONS'] to enable connection timeout
recommendations.rationale: Timeout configuration exists but is unused; prevents connection pooling and timeout protection

recommendations.recommendation: Create custom User model in apps.core immediately after app scaffolding to avoid migration complexity later
recommendations.rationale: Changing to custom User model after initial migrations creates significant data migration burden

recommendations.recommendation: Create spec.md documenting middleware ordering requirements and environment-specific configuration expected behavior
recommendations.rationale: Settings reference spec.md for critical ordering; document should exist to guide team implementation

recommendations.recommendation: Add django-environ, dj-database-url, djangorestframework, djangorestframework-simplejwt to requirements.txt with explicit versions
recommendations.rationale: Settings rely on these packages but don't fail if missing; explicit requirement prevents fallback behavior in production

recommendations.recommendation: Uncomment and configure LOCAL_APPS with proper AppConfig references as apps are created to prevent import issues
recommendations.rationale: Settings prepared for easy app registration; should follow as development progresses
